data.title: High dividend growth + high free cash flow yield stocks = a winning strategy
data.description:
Stocks with high dividend growth and high free cash flow yield can generate more returns than stocks seeing high dividend growth alone, according to a Wolfe Research note.

This defensive strategy has historically outperformed the S&P 500 (SP500) by 500 basis points annually, said Chris Senyek, chief investment strategist.

The following are companies with high dividend growth in a last-twelve-month basis, and a high unlevered free cash flow yield.

  • Twenty-First Century Fox (FOXA) – 2024 FCF yield: 8.9%; Last 12-month dividend growth: 4%
  • The Interpublic Group of Companies (IPG) – 2024 FCF yield: 8.6%; Last 12-month dividend growth: 7%

data.title: A look at real estate stocks after housing inflation moderates, mortgages rise
data.description:
In September's CPI report, the pace of shelter cost increases moderated, slowing to +0.2% from +0.5% in August and +0.4% in July. In other words, housing prices are still rising, but at a slower rate than in the previous two months.

In addition, mortgage rates are climbing. The average rate on a 30-year fixed mortgage rose to 6.32% in the week ended Oct. 10, up from 1.62% in the prior week, its largest one-week jump in almost six months, according to Freddie Mac.

As a result, home affordability remains challenging. The cooling in housing costs is being offset by the increase in mortgage rates. Furthermore, initial jobless claims jumped in the past week, which is not a good sign for the housing market. People without jobs can't afford to buy houses or pay higher rent.

Real estate stocks are leading the broader stock averages lower in Thursday afternoon trading. The Real Estate Sector SPDR ETF (NYSEARCA:XLRE) is falling -1.1%, compared with the S&P 500's -0.4% decline. The iShares U.S. Home Construction ETF (BATS:ITB) slid 1.5%.

Real estate brokers' stocks led the drop. Anywhere Real Estate (NYSE:HOUS) slid 3.0%, Redfin (NASDAQ:RDFN) sank 3.3%, Opendoor Technologies (NASDAQ:OPEN), known for flipping houses, fell 5.0%, and Compass (NYSE:COMP) is down 1.2%. Zillow (NASDAQ:Z) class C shares, though, rose 0.2%.

Homebuilders also felt the pain. D.R. Horton (DHI) stock slipped 1.1%, KB Home (KBH) fell 1.5%, PulteGroup (PHM) dipped 0.6%, Lennar (LEN) -1.2%, Toll Brothers (TOL) -1.8%, and Beazer Homes USA (BZH) -1.4%.

Most home rental REITs climbed during the session. Invitation Homes (INVH) increased 0.4%, AMH (AMH), formerly American Homes 4 Rent,+0.2%, Bluerock Homes Trust (BHM) -0.2%, and NexPoint Diversified Real Estate (NXDT), +2.3%.

Meanwhile, apartment REITs slipped. Equity Residential (EQR) declined 1.0%, Independence Realty Trust (IRT), -1.3%, AvalonBay Communities (AVB) -0.9%, and Camden Property Trust (CPT) -1.5%.

Mortgage lenders were mixed. Rocket Companies (RKT) +0.9%, UWM Holdings (UWMC) -2.2%, and PennyMac Financial Services (PFSI) -0.8%.

data.title: China stimulus: Schwab watching for 3 things that could help sustain Chinese stock gains
data.description:
China made a “move in the right direction” with its recent stimulus measures, said Charles Schwab, adding that it's also looking for three things needed from China to sustain stock market gains.

Chinese stocks soared starting in late September, with China cutting interest rates and taking other actions to bolster the sluggish property market and the economy. Chinese capital markets also landed 800B yuan ($112.99M) in aid. The Shanghai CSI 300 (SHSZ300) surged ~30% before pulling back this week. The MSCI China Index, tracking nearly 600 large-and mid-cap shares and overseas listings, has climbed ~19%. The iShares MSCI China ETF (NASDAQ:MCHI) has advanced nearly 22%.

But Charles Schwab this week noted the lackluster run beforehand in Chinese equities, which has hurt the MSCI Emerging Market Index. The total return of the MSCI Emerging Market Index was a loss of -3% from the end of 2020 until the day before the stimulus announcement came on September 25. Excluding the China Index, the MSCI Emerging Market punches in a 21% gain.

“China's stocks have experienced four stimulus-driven bull markets over the past four years—including the one earlier this year in May—and the prior three all soon fizzled out, reversing their gains,” Jeffrey Kleintop, chief global investment strategist at Charles Schwab, said.

“[There] are a few things we're looking for to judge whether the stimulus announcements deliver the boost to China's growth that may sustain its stock market's gains,” Kleintop said:

Boosting local government special bond issuance

China’s central government last year decided to issue an additional 1T yuan in bonds for the year. Raising the special bond issuance quota by more than 1T yuan would be needed to ease tight fiscal policy, Schwab said.

China's Politburo recently announced 2T yuan in issuance of special treasury bonds. “An announcement on fiscal support for 2024 could come the week of October 22, in conjunction with the Standing Committee meeting,” Kleintop said.

Faster money supply growth in China

Stimulus by the People's Bank of China is aimed at reversing weak borrowing by businesses and consumers to bolster growth. “A sign that this was working would be more demand for money. So far, money supply growth has turned negative in recent months, falling below -5% as of August,” Schwab said.

A rise in consumer and business confidence

Schwab said China could guarantee homebuyer deposits and boost social safety nets to cut down high levels of precautionary savings, and it could make regulatory changes that have stifled business investment.

Consumers "need hope of improvement in income and job growth for the rally to have staying power,” Kleintop said.

Goldman Sachs' data this week showed hedge funds recorded the largest single-day net selling of Chinese equities after China refrained from announcing further stimulus.

The CSI 300 Index (SHSZ300) has risen 11% YTD vs. the S&P 500’s (SP500) rise of 21%.

For investors looking to track China-related stocks through exchange-traded funds, here are a few: (KWEB), (FXI), (GXC), (PGJ), (FLCH), (YINN) and (YANG).

data.title: Oil prices surge 4% on Hurricane Milton, fears of Israel-Iran conflict
data.description:
Oil prices on Thursday rose by 4% as gasoline usage jumped in anticipation of Hurricane Milton’s path through Florida and concerns about Middle East supply. There also were indications that demand for energy would grow in the United States and China.

Brent futures (CO1:COM) rose $3.06, or 4%, to $79.63 a barrel, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate (CL1:COM) crude advanced $2.92, or 4%, to $76.16.

Amid evacuations and power outages, about a quarter of gas stations in Florida sold out of gasoline as Hurricane Milton traversed the state. Prolonged power outages may limit transport of fuel supplies through terminals and pipelines.

Markets also await the possibility that Israel’s military will respond to Iran’s missile attack of last week. Traders are uneasy after Israeli Defense Minister Yoav Gallant on Wednesday said the country’s response “will be deadly, precise and above all surprising,” while Iran has warned it’s ready to launch thousands of missiles if needed.

data.title: Costco is boosted by consumers spending on gold bars, gift cards, and hurricane supplies
data.description:
Costco Wholesale Corporation (NASDAQ:COST) traded lower on Thursday, despite turning in a strong monthly sales performance for September, with a 9.3% core comparable sales tally in the U.S. on 7.6% traffic growth.

Costco (COST) management noted that the September comparable sales mark was boosted by the last week of Hurricane Helene and consumer anxiety over port strikes.

Looking ahead, Jefferies analyst Corey Tarlowe believes Costco (COST) could potentially see a benefit from Hurricane Milton in October. Meanwhile, UBS thinks the retailer is playing its hand well by combining its compelling price/value offering with greater innovation and newness. Analyst Michael Lasser said that has resulted in Costco (COST) customers continuing to consolidate their shopping with it at an increasing pace. "Plus, its enhancements to Costco Logistics delivery platform and digital shopping experience are resulting in sustainably higher digital engagement with its members," he noted.

Oppenheimer pointed out that non-food categories remained a bright spot for Costco (COST) in September, increasing in the low-teens again, fueled by gold bars and gift card spending. Analyst Rupesh Parikh highlighted that Costco (COST) continues to expect strong top-line momentum to persist for the balance of the year, driven by superior merchandising prowess and its unique value proposition. "We also believe COST could see a N-T membership bump related to more stringent membership card checking efforts," he added.

Looking ahead, Costco (COST) continues to innovate. A Costco (COST) warehouse is slated to take up the first floor as an anchor tenant of an 800-unit apartment building in Los Angeles in what marks a first for the company in establishing a presence in a mixed-used building. The smaller-sized store, which will have pharmacy and optical departments, could be a precursor to a new format for Costco (COST).

Shares of Costco (COST) were down 1.74% in late afternoon trading to cut into the +30% year-to-date gain.

data.title: BioMarin resumed at Raymond James at outperform as stock price has hit floor
data.description:
• Raymond James has reinstated coverage of BioMarin Pharmaceutical (NASDAQ:BMRN) with an outperform rating saying that shares are now oversold despite upcoming competition for Voxzogo (vosoritide) for achondroplasia.
  • The firm has a $79 price target (~13% upside based on Oct. 9 close).
  • Although analyst Danielle Brill sees competition such as Ascendis Pharma's (ASND) TransCon CNP eventually taking over 95% of the achondroplasia market, she sees strong revenues in the future from enzyme products and Roctavian (valoctocogene roxaparvovec) for hemophilia A of, respectively, $3B and $230M in 2033.
  • She noted that if the Voxzogo total addressable market doubles and BioMarin divides the achondroplasia market equally with competitors, a more bullish case and price target of $110 could be made.
  • BioMarin is down ~28% year to date.

data.title: Wolfe’s tax loss selling stocks within industrials, tech, materials
data.description:
Wolfe Research put together a tax loss selling basket.

Here is part 1.

Typically, the biggest year-to-date losers underperform in a big way during the last months of the year, and with the Oct. 31 mutual fund year-end approaching, analysts expect tax loss selling to accelerate.

“Historically, avoiding the biggest year-to-date losers has been a positive alpha generating strategy heading into the last several months of the year,” wrote Chris Senyek, chief investment strategist, in a note. “The reason is that the market’s biggest laggards can be subject to selling pressures by investors looking to harvest capital losses and/or provide ‘window dressing’ for their annual reports.”

September, October and December are the most common for mutual funds.

The following stocks, divided by sector, are in Wolfe’s tax loss selling basket, part 2:

Industrials:

  • Avis Budget Group Inc. (CAR) – Year-to-date price change: -53%; Price vs. 12-month volume-weighted average price: -32%

data.title: WW surge after GLP-1 compounding announcement not justified after study, Barclays says
HP Enterprise unveils new ProLiant Compute XD685 system powered by AMD AI
data.description:
• HP Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) held its first ever artificial intelligence day and the company made several announcements, including a new server powered by AMD's (NASDAQ:AMD) AI chips.
  • The HPE ProLiant Compute XD685 will allow for complex AI model training tasks, thanks to AMD's Instinct MI325X accelerators.
  • HP Enterprise Chief Technology Officer Fidelma Russo said at the event in Wisconsin that generative AI and inferencing at the edge can be aided with on-premise and hybrid solutions, and HP Enterprise is poised to aid, with its hybrid approach.
  • “Our collaboration with HPE continues to deliver flexible, highly performant solutions that help maximize AI efforts for increased demand in large language model training and advances competitive industry innovation,” AMD data center executive Forrest Norrod said in a statement.

This is breaking news...

data.title: Alcon upgraded to neutral by Redburn, operational execution cited
data.description:
• Redburn upgraded Alcon (NYSE:ALC) to neutral from sell, commenting that the company’s operational execution has surpassed its expectations.
  • The investment firm said it believes a new equipment launch cycle can accelerate growth in the company’s surgical business, with a “halo effect” supporting growth in its cataract business.
  • Redburn also sees Alcon’s (ALC) PanOptix launch supporting its market share in implantables in the mid-term.
  • “While mix pressures remain into FY 2025, consensus margin forecasts appear realistic," the firm added.
  • Redburn noted, however, that the stock’s “premium valuation limits potential upside.”
